text.title: AI-Assisted Coding Protocol for Qualitative Data Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-coding-of-qualitative-data-protocol methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Codebook MUST live in source control with semver tags; every coding run records the codebook version used.
rule.rationale: Without versioning, results across passes mix codebook states and kappa numbers become meaningless.

rule.statement: Inter-coder agreement (Cohen's kappa) MUST reach ≥0.7 on a 10% sample before final coding ships; lower triggers codebook revision.
rule.rationale: Kappa < 0.7 indicates codes are under-defined; downstream analysis built on it is fragile.

rule.statement: Every coded segment MUST record (segment_id, code_ids, coder, codebook_version, timestamp).
rule.rationale: Audit trail is the only defense in peer review or compliance audit.

rule.statement: AI MUST propose codes; humans reconcile disagreements and update codebook; AI never finalises without human sign-off.
rule.rationale: AI alone over-fits to recent context and drifts; human reconciliation is the calibration step.

rule.statement: If corpus <20 segments OR no second coder, skip and use manual coding.
rule.rationale: Methodology amortizes only above corpus threshold and inter-coder check is meaningless without 2 coders.
